title: vaccinium commutatum
intro: Vaccinium commutatum, also known as the bog blueberry or common bog blueberry, is a low-growing, woody shrub that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Vaccinium commutatum include its potential to help protect against cell damage, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Vaccinium commutatum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves, stems, and roots can be used in herbal preparations to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
